title: Inspired by its anti-Belichick coach, Paraguay forged a famous win in Foxborough
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany in a penalty shootout in the World Cup Round of 32 at Gillette Stadium.
Paraguayan coach Gustavo Alfaro described his team's victory as the greatest accomplishment of his career.
Germany missed three penalty kicks in the shootout, an unprecedented event in their World Cup history.

title: Jaylen Brown has reportedly ‘grown frustrated’ with Celtics amid offseason rife with trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is reportedly frustrated with the Celtics amid ongoing trade rumors this summer.
The Celtics continue to field trade offers for Brown, but it's unclear if they are actively looking to trade him.
Brown expressed a desire to remain with the Celtics, despite rumors connecting him to potential trades.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras sheds tears, gets ejected in emotional night at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a three-run homer in Red Sox's 6-3 win over the Nationals at Fenway Park.
Contreras was emotional due to recent devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, dedicating his home run to his country.
Contreras was ejected for challenging a non-reviewable check-swing call, causing further controversy and frustration.

title: Are these Boston-area colleges really $100K a year? It’s complicated.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Some Boston-area college costs exceed $100,000 annually, but most students pay less due to financial aid and scholarships.
Sticker prices include tuition, fees, room, and board, but actual student costs often differ significantly from published amounts.
Colleges often use endowments and tuition from wealthier families to subsidize lower-income students through financial aid.

title: 2 Ariana Grande concerts at TD Garden are rescheduled
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ariana Grande's TD Garden concerts on July 22 and July 24 rescheduled to July 23 and July 26 respectively.
TD Garden announced production challenges prompted the changes to ensure safe and intended delivery of the shows.
Tickets for the rescheduled dates will be honored, and ticket holders will receive direct communication with further details.

title: Bruins prospect Dean Letourneau has gone from a project to a legitimate blue-chip talent
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau scored 22 goals in his sophomore year at Boston College, improving from zero goals in his freshman year.
The 6-foot-7 Bruins prospect was a 2024 first-round draft pick and is now a leading talent in their pipeline.
Letourneau aims to dominate in his next college season before potentially advancing to the pro ranks.

title: Why did City Hall’s FIFA Fan Festival close with some World Cup games left to play?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston's FIFA Fan Festival at City Hall closed after the World Cup group stage, attracting over 155,000 visitors.
Financial constraints led to the festival's closure despite FIFA initially requiring festivals for the entire tournament duration.
Mayor Wu is exploring additional public viewing options in Boston for remaining World Cup matches after the Fan Fest closure.

title: Could Bruins be a viable landing spot for Darnell Nurse via trade?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Darnell Nurse, Oilers defenseman with a $9.25 million cap hit, has requested a trade after 12 seasons in Edmonton.
The Boston Bruins need defensive reinforcements and Nurse could boost their defense, specifically pairing with Charlie McAvoy.
Bruins face salary cap challenges with $7.7 million space, potentially trading players to acquire Nurse and address defensive weaknesses.

title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car with a family of three was retrieved from the Seekonk River near Taft Street boat ramp in Pawtucket, Rhode Island.
A jet ski rider heard the car enter the water and attempted rescue; another witness called 911 immediately.
Efforts involved Pawtucket and Rhode Island State Police, facing difficult dive conditions due to strong currents and poor visibility.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry has closed its Downtown Boston restaurant and will focus on catering and its food truck.
The food truck will stop at Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common weekly during the summer.
Chef Ernie Campbell founded Jamaica Mi Hungry in 2012, expanding from catering to food trucks and restaurants.
